summary: Clear soundboard capture of a tight December ’71 Felt Forum show featuring stellar versions of Truckin’, Loser, The Other One jam and a crisp Mr. Charlie.
review: The performance is widely praised for its tightness and intricate jamming, with highlights like “Truckin’”, “Loser”, a long “The Other One > B McGee > The Other One” suite, and an especially clear rendition of “Mr. Charlie”. Recording quality is generally described as flawless or crisp soundboard, though a few listeners note occasional piano distortion and kick‑drum buzz.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers gave 4–5 stars and praised both the musical performance and sound quality; only a few mentioned minor audio flaws, yielding an overall strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and crisp soundboard capture; occasional distorted piano mic (Keith) and slight kick‑drum buzz noted by a few reviewers.

standout_songs: The Other One > B McGee > The Other One
show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers describe the set as tight, well‑structured and never boring, with seamless transitions into long improvisational jams that showcase the band’s cohesion at this point in their evolution.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring solos, taking spotlight on ballads, and strong interplay during jams.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted as part of the “hook‑up” with Jerry and Keith during the extended jam; solid bass foundation.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano/organ work highlighted as integral to the sound; occasional mic distortion mentioned but overall praised.
band_member_comments.Drums: Generally tight playing, though a few reviewers heard buzz on the kick drum.
